title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk; however, widespread adoption without proper vetting could lead to false positives, missed vulnerabilities, or exploitation if the tool contains flaws. It may also expose sensitive dependency data if misconfigured.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's credibility and security before integrating it into workflows.
Conduct regular manual audits alongside automated scans to ensure comprehensive vulnerability detection.
Monitor community feedback and updates for potential issues or improvements in the tool.

title: A vulnerability was identified in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. This affects the...
description: A vulnerability was identified in Totolink A7100RU 7.4cu.2313_b20191024. This affects the function setSyslogCfg of the file /cgi-bin/cstecgi.cgi of the component CGI Handler. Such manipulation of the argument enable leads to os command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Totolink A7100RU allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands via the setSyslogCfg function in the CGI handler. This could lead to full system compromise, unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or use of the device in larger botnet attacks.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Totolink A7100RU firmware to the latest version if a patch is available. If no patch exists, contact the vendor for guidance.
Restrict access to the device's management interface by implementing IP whitelisting, VPNs, or other network segmentation techniques.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and consider disabling the vulnerable function if possible until a fix is applied.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Tenda AC9 15.03.02.13. Impacted is the function formQuickIndex...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Tenda AC9 15.03.02.13. Impacted is the function formQuickIndex of the file /goform/QuickIndex of the component POST Request Handler. Such manipulation of the argument PPPOEPassword leads to stack-based buffer overflow. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

title: A vulnerability was detected in D-Link DIR-513 1.10. This vulnerability affects the function...
description: A vulnerability was detected in D-Link DIR-513 1.10. This vulnerability affects the function formSetRoute of the file /goform/formSetRoute of the component POST Request Handler. The manipulation of the argument curTime results in buffer overflow. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit is now public and may be used. This vulnerability only affects products that are no longer supported by the maintainer.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the D-Link DIR-513 1.10 firmware allows for remote exploitation via a buffer overflow in the formSetRoute function. This could lead to unauthorized remote code execution, system compromise, or network disruption. The exploit is public, increasing the likelihood of active exploitation.
recommended_actions: Immediately replace or decommission affected D-Link DIR-513 devices, as they are no longer supported and will not receive security patches.
Implement network segmentation to isolate vulnerable devices from critical systems and sensitive data.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity and apply intrusion detection/prevention systems to detect potential exploitation attempts.

title: A flaw has been found in code-projects Patient Record Management System 1.0. The affected element...
description: A flaw has been found in code-projects Patient Record Management System 1.0. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /hematology_print.php. Executing a manipulation of the argument hem_id can lead to sql injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in the Patient Record Management System 1.0 could allow attackers to manipulate database queries, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data leakage, or data corruption. This poses a significant risk to sensitive patient data and system integrity.
recommended_actions: Immediately patch the application by updating to a version that addresses CVE-2026-6005 or apply vendor-provided fixes.
Implement input validation and sanitization for the 'hem_id' parameter to prevent SQL injection vulnerabilities.
Conduct a comprehensive security audit of the application to identify and remediate other potential vulnerabilities.

title: The installer certificate files in the …/bootstrap/common/ssl folder do not seem to have...
description: The installer certificate files in the …/bootstrap/common/ssl folder do not seem to have restricted permissions on Windows systems (users have read and execute access). For the client.key file in particular, this could potentially lead to exploits, as this exposes agent identity material to any locally authenticated standard user.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The lack of restricted permissions on installer certificate files, particularly the client.key file, could allow locally authenticated users to access sensitive agent identity material, potentially leading to impersonation or unauthorized access.
recommended_actions: Restrict permissions on the …/bootstrap/common/ssl folder and its contents to limit access to only necessary users and processes.
Audit systems for unauthorized access to the client.key file and other sensitive materials, and rotate compromised keys if necessary.
Implement monitoring and alerting mechanisms to detect any unauthorized access attempts to sensitive files.

title: wolfSSL_X509_verify_cert in the OpenSSL compatibility layer accepts a certificate chain in which...
description: wolfSSL_X509_verify_cert in the OpenSSL compatibility layer accepts a certificate chain in which the leaf's signature is not checked, if the attacker supplies an untrusted intermediate with Basic Constraints `CA:FALSE` that is legitimately signed by a trusted root. An attacker who obtains any leaf certificate from a trusted CA (e.g. a free DV cert from Let's Encrypt) can forge a certificate for any subject name with any public key and arbitrary signature bytes, and the function returns `WOLFSSL_

title: An integer overflow existed in the wolfCrypt CMAC implementation, that could be exploited to...
description: An integer overflow existed in the wolfCrypt CMAC implementation, that could be exploited to forge CMAC tags. The function wc_CmacUpdate used the guard `if (cmac->totalSz != 0)` to skip XOR-chaining on the first block (where digest is all-zeros and the XOR is a no-op). However, totalSz is word32 and wraps to zero after 2^28 block flushes (4 GiB), causing the guard to erroneously discard the live CBC-MAC chain state. Any two messages sharing a common suffix beyond the 4 GiB mark then produce iden

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5477.

Executing a manipulation can lead to server-side request forgery. The attack can be executed remotely. This attack is characterized by high complexity. The exploitation is known to be difficult. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading to version 2

title: wolfSSL's wc_PKCS7_DecodeAuthEnvelopedData() does not properly sanitize the AES-GCM...
description: wolfSSL's wc_PKCS7_DecodeAuthEnvelopedData() does not properly sanitize the AES-GCM authentication tag length received and has no lower bounds check. A man-in-the-middle can therefore truncate the mac field from 16 bytes to 1 byte, reducing the tag check from 2⁻¹²⁸ to 2⁻⁸.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5500.

title: The AddFunc Head & Footer Code plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting...
description: The AddFunc Head & Footer Code plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the `aFhfc_head_code`, `aFhfc_body_code`, and `aFhfc_footer_code` post meta values in all versions up to, and including, 2.3. This is due to the plugin outputting these meta values without any sanitization or escaping. While the plugin restricts its own metabox and save handler to administrators via `current_user_can('manage_options')`, it does not use `register_meta()` with an `auth_callback` to

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to inject malicious scripts into the website's head, body, or footer sections via stored cross-site scripting (XSS). This could lead to unauthorized data access, session hijacking, or the delivery of malicious payloads to site visitors. The impact is limited to administrators with 'manage_options' permissions, but if an admin account is compromised, the damage could be significant.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or patch the plugin to a version where this vulnerability is addressed, if available.
Implement a temporary fix by disabling or removing the AddFunc Head & Footer Code plugin until a secure version is released.
Restrict access to administrator accounts and enforce strong authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA), to minimize the risk of exploitation.

title: An integer underflow issue exists in wolfSSL when parsing the Subject Alternative Name (SAN)...
description: An integer underflow issue exists in wolfSSL when parsing the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) extension of X.509 certificates. A malformed certificate can specify an entry length larger than the enclosing sequence, causing the internal length counter to wrap during parsing. This results in incorrect handling of certificate data. The issue is limited to configurations using the original ASN.1 parsing implementation which is off by default.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The integer underflow issue in wolfSSL's ASN.1 parsing implementation could allow attackers to craft malformed certificates that bypass proper validation, potentially leading to unauthorized access or data interception. However, the impact is limited as the vulnerable configuration is off by default.
recommended_actions: Verify if the affected ASN.1 parsing implementation is enabled in your wolfSSL configuration and disable it if not required.
Update to the latest version of wolfSSL where the issue has been patched.
Conduct a review of certificate validation processes to ensure compliance with security best practices.

title: wolfSSL's ECCSI signature verifier `wc_VerifyEccsiHash` decodes the `r` and `s` scalars from the...
description: wolfSSL's ECCSI signature verifier `wc_VerifyEccsiHash` decodes the `r` and `s` scalars from the signature blob via `mp_read_unsigned_bin` with no check that they lie in `[1, q-1]`. A crafted forged signature could verify against any message for any identity, using only publicly-known constants.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5466.

title: In wolfSSL's EVP layer, the ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD decryption path in wolfSSL_EVP_CipherFinal ...
description: In wolfSSL's EVP layer, the ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD decryption path in wolfSSL_EVP_CipherFinal (and related EVP cipher finalization functions) fails to verify the authentication tag before returning plaintext to the caller. When an application uses the EVP API to perform ChaCha20-Poly1305 decryption, the implementation computes or accepts the tag but does not compare it against the expected value.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in wolfSSL's ChaCha20-Poly1305 AEAD decryption path allows attackers to bypass authentication checks, potentially leading to unauthorized data decryption or tampering. This could compromise the confidentiality and integrity of sensitive data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to the latest patched version of wolfSSL that addresses CVE-2026-5479.
Audit all applications and systems using wolfSSL's EVP API for ChaCha20-Poly1305 decryption to assess potential exposure.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect any suspicious decryption activities until the vulnerability is fully mitigated.

title: CVE-2026-35539 - An issue was discovered in Roundcube Webmail before 1.5.14 and 1.6.14. XSS exists because of insuffi
description: An issue was discovered in Roundcube Webmail before 1.5.14 and 1.6.14. XSS exists because of insufficient HTML attachment sanitization in preview mode. A victim must preview a text/html attachment.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of the user's browser session by exploiting insufficient sanitization of HTML attachments in preview mode. This could lead to session hijacking, data theft, or further exploitation.
recommended_actions: Update Roundcube Webmail to version 1.5.14 or 1.6.14 immediately to patch the vulnerability.
Implement additional input sanitization measures on HTML attachments at the server level to prevent potential exploitation.
Educate users about the risks of previewing untrusted email attachments and encourage caution when handling emails from unknown sources.
